reviews.review_msg: Loved his lectures, very engaging & had meaningful class discussions. Applies materials learned in class to outside life experiences which help you understand better. Would highly recommend.

Although professor Jin is very kind and knowledgable his accent makes it near impossible to understand a word he says. There is also little to no information/guidelines for all assignments. He is a generous grader but the class itself was hard to follow, I don't think I really learnt anything.

By far the best communications prof in my 3 years as a communications student. Lectures are amazing, takes the readings and breaks it down into relatable examples. Highly respected scholar in the communication field, would highly recommend taking his class if you are a comm student!

He cannot speak english at a proficient enough level to teach at an english university.  It is impossible for me to understand how somebody who has been teaching in english for so many years has an accent that is so unintelligible.  I can't even fathom how he was hired.  Lectures are dry, boring, and mandatory.  Avoid if possible.
Had her in Spring 2020. Easy class. She was really straightforward with her lecture. I am a fan of her teaching style.

Great teacher overall and he is always available to help when needed. His grading scale is higher than usual communications grading however he marks easier to it. You should come prepared to class by doing readings. His slides are really informative and useful, it is really easy to understand too. I love this class so much and would def retake it

Jin is an excellent prof who really knows his stuff. I think his only downfall is his thick accent, which is a shame because he is an incredibly bright and intelligent guy and it shows in his writing. But the lectures were so enjoyable, despite being difficult to understand. I really did enjoy the class though!

He's awesome! Super funny and kind. Has a thick accent but you get used to it. He speaks English fluently so there is no "language barrier." He's also a very prolific scholar always publishing pieces relevant to the courses he teaches (in English AND Korean). He's also very helpful and wants students to succeed. He doesn't do email very well though
Dal is much respected by his students. Though there is a language barrier he makes great, informative slides that basically sum up readings. Would definitely take his class again  :)
can be hard to understand sometimes and can mumble but just bear with him! His slides are packed with information. Always willing to help.
You can learn a lot from professor Jin, and he is very approachable in class. He is smart, funny, and caring. However, outside of class, he seems very busy about his life

Professor Jin is awesome! He is a really nice, a great marker, and can be quite generous, CMNS 348 was an easy class to do well in, and achieve a high mark with some effort. As mentioned before, he has an accent, which I was worried about at first, but after the first couple of weeks you become use to it and he is easy to understand. Take it!

He is an expert in his fields of study, generous and humorous, not a tough grader definitely, and he's a very flexible person. I liked this prof so much and the contexts of his lecture were pretty interesting too! recommended!

I really liked Dal's class. The accent can be hard to get used to at first but it's fine after the first few classes. You definitely have to attend, but the format is engaging and he's an extremely fair marker. If you do the work an A should be achievable. He also assigns his own readings and he's extremely knowledgable.

Jin was one of the best prof I encountered at SFU. He does have an accent, but he always emphases his points and his PPTs are uploaded every week. (Around week3, I got used to the accent). Attendance is a must. Midterm was easy. Jin is very knowledgable on the subject and he is also a very democratic prof. He genuinely cares about students.

Jin was a great prof! At times his accent was a bit hard to understand but he always repeated the important points more than once and he put his slides up every week so I never had any problem with that. He is very passionate about what he teaches and has a great sense of humor! Just make sure you're always on time, he doesn't like late-comers.

He uses perfect English but his thick accent makes it hard to understand him at first. He's very friendly, smiles often, and his materials were interesting. He started posting lecture notes online too which is awesome. He even wrote some of the assigned readings himself (as he's a writer too).

He's very hard to understand. Honestly not sure how some has an accent that thick considering he has taught in Canada and the US for many years (according to him). Stumbles along through his speech as well, so makes it worse. You must listen intently. Overall a good prof though, fair and easy to do well.
Nice prof. Knowledgeable and helpful.However, very severe. Do not miss a class. Slides are not online and the course is not recorded. Fair marker.

Loved this professor until I realized how absolutely unreasonable he can be. If you are a busy working student, DO NOT take his class because he has no consideration for genuine reasons provided for an extension. Very disappointed in the end.

His thick accent makes him REALLY hard to understand during lecture, plus he stutters quite a bit and repeats himself. I appreciated his lecture notes though - clear & understandable... with perfect English. Class was alright. I liked that he often used popular ideas as examples, ie he showed the Gangnam Style MV in class once.

The course material is way too broad, but Jin is a really nice person; the course is pretty self-explanatory, and there's not much to go to him for (he does drift off-topic in lecture and in conversation). As for his English, he does have a slight accent, but his writing is clear and easy to understand, and no reason not to comprehend his lectures.
his lecture is boring and useless.
He's linear with the marking, nice person. But he's accent is hard to understand sometimes...
deadadadaaaa is all i have to say. great and sweet prof! take his class :)
nice prof. the materials were way too broad unfortunately
He knows his stuff but never tries to help out students. Sometimes hard to understand his accent
Jin is a great prof who wants his students to do well.  He presents really interesting stuff sometimes but not always.
Jin definately knows his stuff, he does have a thick accent but if you sit near the fron its fine. His tests are fair as long as you attend lectures.
Very nice guy, and very knowledgeable.  A little hard to understand at times, but not too bad.  Not a critical course at all, a lot of history and information.

Mr. Jin is a fantastic teacher. He simplifies the concepts clearly for students. His exams are straightforward if you attend his lectures. His notes are thorough. Despite his accent, he speaks better English than most English teachers. If in doubt, take any class that he teaches. He definitely thinks about how to teach so that students learn.
